last_result.description: Run a proxy same-EF architecture comparison at `K=40`, `EF=8` with `ARCHITECTURE=jumprelu`, holding `signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0` fixed to test whether a different sparse activation mechanism improves the lower-K frontier where several TopK-style small-EF families have failed.
last_result.self_review: This keeps the round on a fundamentally different architecture path under the same lower-K and same-EF comparison target, avoids reopening minor parameter sweeps, and directly tests whether the main failure mode is the TopK mechanism itself. The main risk is that `jumprelu` also underperforms badly at `K=40`, but that would further support that the current small-EF lower-K regime is fundamentally misaligned and may require a broader reformulation rather than more local search.
